title: NASA's New Trip to the Moon
summary: NASA is sending astronauts back to the Moon with the Artemis II mission. This mission will help prepare for future Moon landings and building a base there. The Moon has important resources like water, metals, and rare elements that can be used for living and exploring space. Water is especially useful because it can be turned into air to breathe and fuel for rockets. NASA wants to go back to learn more and use these resources, unlike the old Apollo missions that were about racing to space. This new journey involves many people and costs a lot of money, but it aims to help humans live and work on the Moon someday.

the big rock that orbits Earth and is our closest neighbor in space

a liquid that is important for drinking and can be split into air and fuel
things like water and metals that can be used to help people live and explore
the name of NASA's mission to send astronauts around the Moon

questions.question: What is NASA planning to do with the Artemis II mission?
Why is water important on the Moon?
options: It can be used for drinking and turned into air and fuel
It makes the Moon look pretty
It is only found in craters
questions.correct_answer: It can be used for drinking and turned into air and fuel

questions.question: What resources does the Moon have besides water?
How is the Artemis programme different from the old Apollo missions?
options: It focuses on using resources and building a base, not just racing
It only studies the Moon from Earth
questions.correct_answer: It focuses on using resources and building a base, not just racing

questions.question: What can water on the Moon be split into?
Why does NASA want to go back to the Moon?
options: To learn more and use resources for future living
To win a race with another country
questions.correct_answer: To learn more and use resources for future living

background_read: The Moon is Earth's closest neighbor in space, and people first landed there over 50 years ago with NASA's Apollo missions. Now, NASA is starting a new program called Artemis to return astronauts to the Moon. This time, the goal is not just to visit but to stay longer by using things found on the Moon, like water and metals. Water is special because it can help astronauts breathe and power rockets. Learning about the Moon can help us explore more of space in the future.
Article_Structure: WHAT: Sending astronauts to the Moon with Artemis II to prepare for a base and use resources
WHY: To learn more, use resources like water and metals, and help humans live on the Moon someday

perspectives.description: NASA is going back to study the Moon's resources and environment to advance space science.
